task: I need to brief my team before the next shift about a process change, and I want to make sure the message is clear enough for a busy room. Can you help me draft a version I could use in a quick huddle, and a backup line in case I only have a few seconds?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — I can help with that.

If you send me:
- **What the process change is**
- **Who it affects**
- **What people need to do differently**
- **Any deadline or start time**
- **Any key reason or risk to mention**

I’ll draft:
1. a **short huddle version** that’s clear and easy to say out loud, and  
2. a **very short backup line** for when you only have a few seconds.

If you want, I can also make it sound:
- **formal**
- **friendly**
- **firm / urgent**
- **super simple for a mixed-experience team**

user_simulator.text:
Fine. Use this:

Process change: starting next shift, every new account and refinance file gets a second check before it leaves the desk. No skipping it, even if the line is backed up.

Who it affects: everyone handling openings, loans, and follow-up docs.

What changes: before you hand it off, make sure the ID, signatures, income docs, and notes are complete. If anything is missing, fix it right then.

Reason: we already got burned once on loose files. I’m not doing that again.

Quick huddle version:
‘Starting next shift, every new account and refinance file needs a full second check before it leaves your desk. That means ID, signatures, income docs, and notes all have to be complete. If something’s missing, stop and fix it before you move on. No rushing it out the door. We’re tightening this up because incomplete files slow us down and create problems later.’

Backup line:
‘New rule next shift: double-check every file before it leaves your desk. No missing docs, no shortcuts.’

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a polished version you can use in a busy huddle, plus a very short backup line.

### Quick huddle version
“Starting next shift, every new account and refinance file needs a second check before it leaves your desk. That means making sure the ID, signatures, income docs, and notes are all complete. If anything is missing, stop and fix it right then before you hand it off. No skipping this step, even if we’re backed up. We’re tightening this up because incomplete files cause problems later.”

### Backup line
“Next shift: every file gets a second check before it leaves your desk. No missing docs, no shortcuts.”

If you want, I can also make it sound a little more **firm**, **friendly**, or **more boss-style**.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. Clean and straight to the point. I can use that in a huddle without it turning into a speech.
